overview: Over the past 10 years (2016-2025), PSLE English Oral SBC topics heavily feature relatable, everyday student experiences, with "School life / CCAs", "Hobbies / leisure activities", and "Time management / responsibility" dominating the exams. The prompts consistently assess a student's ability to reflect on personal habits, interpersonal relationships, and civic duties. Recently, there has been a noticeable shift towards broader societal themes, such as "Public conduct / civic-mindedness" (2024, 2025) and "Technology & Future-oriented topics" (2022, 2023), reflecting Singapore's evolving landscape. Conversely, while "Recycling / environmental responsibility" is a major global issue, it has surprisingly only appeared once (2022). Topics like financial literacy or mental health have never been explicitly tested, as the focus remains firmly on actionable, age-appropriate moral and social values.
prediction: "Recycling / environmental responsibility" is highly likely to appear in the next PSLE Oral exam, as it only appeared once in 2022 and sustainability is a growing national focus. Additionally, "Reading / lifelong learning" has not been tested since 2021, making it overdue for a return, possibly framed around digital literacy or new ways of learning.
